description: Got a tarball at /home/user/backups/site-2024-03-15.tar.gz that I need to extract to /home/user/restore/ but only the config files — everything under etc/ in the archive. Don't need the whole thing, it's like 2GB of logs and uploads I don't care about right now.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/backups/site-2024-03-15.tar.gz exists (gzipped tarball, ~50MB).
- Archive structure:
    var/www/html/index.php
    var/www/html/uploads/ (many files)
    var/log/apache2/access.log (large)
    var/log/apache2/error.log
    etc/apache2/apache2.conf
    etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf
    etc/php/8.1/apache2/php.ini
- /home/user/restore/ directory exists but is empty.
- /home/user/backups/ and /home/user/restore/ are writable.
- tar, gzip available. Standard Ubuntu 22.04.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/restore/etc/apache2/apache2.conf exists with correct content.
- /home/user/restore/etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf exists with correct content.
- /home/user/restore/etc/php/8.1/apache2/php.ini exists with correct content.
- All three files match their content in the original archive byte-for-byte.

Invariants:
- /home/user/restore/var/ must NOT exist (no extraction of var/www or var/log).
- /home/user/backups/site-2024-03-15.tar.gz unchanged.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `find /home/user/restore -type f | wc -l` should return exactly 3.
- `test -d /home/user/restore/var && echo FAIL || echo OK` should print OK.
